Chest Pain.title: Chest Pain
Chest Pain.intro:
Case Introduction

Day 1 @ 16:00
Emergency Department
A 65-year-old white man is brought in to the emergency department because of sharp chest pain and difficulty breathing. At bedside, he currently is in acute distress, and is moaning and holding his hands over the right side of his chest
Chest Pain.vitals:
Initial Vital Signs

Day 1 @ 16:00

Temperature:

37.0 C (98.6 degrees F)

Pulse:

120 beats/min, Weak

Respiratory rate:

34 /minute

Blood pressure, systolic:

100 mm Hg

Blood pressure, diastolic:

60 mm Hg

Height:

183cm (72.0 in)

Weight:

97.5 kg (215 lb)

Body Mass Index:

29.1 kg/m2
Chest Pain.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Chest pain; respiratory distress
History of present Illness:
The patient, a 65-year-old accountant, is brought to the emergency department by ambulance from the trucking company, where he works. Ten minutes prior to the ambulance’s arrival, the patient developed excruciating, sharp pain on the right side of his chest accompanied by marked respiratory distress. He rates the pain as an 8 on a 10-point scale. The pain increases on respiration, and he is unable to answer questions. A coworker who accompanied the patient to the hospital says that this has never happened to the patient in the past. Coworker mentions patient has a past medical history of emphysema and asthma. Oxygen was administered during transport.

Altered Mental Status.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Altered mental status
History of present Illness:
A 70-year-old Caucasian man comes to the Emergency room because his family members are concerned that something is wrong. The patient has been having behavioral changes over a period of three or four weeks. He used to be very active and managed all of his financial affairs. He now barely talks, stares at the wall for hours at a time, and sleeps most of the day. Four weeks ago, the patient got up to urinate and fell before reaching the bathroom. He was found on the floor the next morning by family members. He was taken to his family doctor and was discharged with a prescription to buy a cane. Since that time, he has been having problems walking. There have been no fevers or night sweats. The patient is currently in no pain. 

Past Medical History: Chronic alcohol abuse; cataracts.

Hospitalization Procedures: Hemorrhoidectomy 1 year ago. Last colonoscopy was 4 years ago which demonstrated no abnormalities.

Current medications: Aspirin 81mg PO daily

Allergies: None

Vaccinations: Up to date

Family History:
Father: Deceased due to a 'heart attack'.
Mother: Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus and died of natural causes. 
The patient has no siblings.

Social History: Marital/Family: Widowed; he has three children. Personal habits: the patient smokes 1 Pack/day. He currently does not drink alcohol but was a chronic alcoholic for 30 years. Denies use of illegal drugs or misuse of medications.

Occupation/educational: He is a retired Army human resources manager. He is a high school graduate.

Recreational: The patient likes to spend time with his children and enjoys collecting stamps.

Review of systems:
General: See HPI
Skin: Negative
HEENT: Negative
Cardiorespiratory: Negative
Gastrointestinal: Negative
Genitourinary: Negative
Neuropsychiatric: See HPI

--- Chart tabs ---

* Chart: Progress Notes → presentation_2_Altered_Mental_Status_chart_Progress_Notes.png
* Chart: Vital Signs → presentation_2_Altered_Mental_Status_chart_Vital_Signs.png

Pelvic Pain.title: Pelvic Pain
Pelvic Pain.intro:
Case Introduction

Day 1 @ 9:00
Office
A 20-year-old female presents to your office with pelvic pain which has been occurring for the last month.

Weight:

65.8 kg (145 lb)

Body Mass Index:

21.4 kg/m2
Pelvic Pain.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Pelvic pain
History of present Illness:
This is a 20-year-old previously healthy female presenting to your office complaining of pelvic pain for the last month. She rates the pain as a constant 7/10 sharp pain which worsens when she moves. She was encouraged by her boyfriend to come to you because she is having extreme pain during sex. Ibuprofen "takes the edge off." The pain is severe enough that she reports having difficulty sleeping. Her pain worsens during her menstruation and that the pain is "unbearable." She admits that she has had unprotected sex with her boyfriend a few times, but reports that she is taking an oral contraceptive. She denies multiple sexual partners in the past. She has had a few episodes of vomiting and still feels nauseous. No fevers or chills. She also reports pain when having a bowel movement. She denies any vaginal discharge. 

Past Medical History: 
Current medications: Oral Contraceptive
Allergies: None
Vaccinations: Received Childhood vaccinations.
Women's health: She has never been to a gynecologist. 
Family History: The patient's mother has a history of breast cancer in her 50s. Her father had a 'heart attack' in his 40s. 

Marital/Family: The patient is the oldest of 3 children. Both of her siblings are healthy without medical problems. 
Personal habits: She admits to smoking 2 cigarettes a day to help lose weight. She also admits to smoking marijuana occasionally at parties. She also drinks alcohol once a week with friends. 
Occupation/educational: She is a college student and currently studying Art History and would like to be a teacher. 
Recreational: She enjoys to paint and train dogs. 

Review of systems:
General: See HPI
Skin: Negative
HEENT: Negative
Cardiorespiratory: Negative
Gastrointestinal: See HPI
Genitourinary: Negative
Neuropsychiatric: Negative

Abdominal Pain.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Abdominal Pain
History of present Illness:
A 22-year-old female presents to the emergency room with abdominal pain, lethargy, and vomiting x2 days. Her vomiting is non-bloody. Her abdominal pain is diffuse, which she rates as a 6/10. She also claims to have had an increased appetite prior to having these symptoms. She also claims that she’s been developing blurry vision, dizziness, and drowsiness. She also states that she is having increased urination

Past Medical History

Past Medical History: None
Current medications: Birth control
Allergies: None
Vaccinations: The patient received her childhood vaccinations. She has never received any HPV vaccines.
Women's health: The patient cannot remember when her last pap smear was done.
Family History: The patient has a brother with Diabetes. 

Social History:
Marital/Family: Single. She is an undergraduate student at a local university.
Personal habits: The patient denies smoking. She drinks socially. She smoked marijuana "a few times" with her friends. She denies other illicit drugs or misuse of medications. Occupation/educational: She is a student at a local university
Recreational: She plays the piano and sings in a band. 

Review of systems:
General: See HPI
Skin: Her skin is dry. 
HEENT: Her throat is dry. 
Cardiorespiratory: She feels that her heart is beating faster.
Gastrointestinal: See HPI
Genitourinary: See HPI
Neuropsychiatric: See HPI

Headache.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Headache
History of present Illness:
A 55-year-old female with a past medical history of hypertension presents to your office with a an occipital headache, mild vertigo, and a tendency to fall when walking for 6 days. She states that she never had any problems walking or being off-balance. Eight days ago, she stated that she had a pink rash on her arms and torso and felt feverish. The rash improved after a couple days, but the headache and tendency to fall persisted. She had nausea, but she denied any vomiting. She feels weak and "under the weather."  She denies travel, but went hiking 2 weeks ago nearby her house which she routinely does every summer. She denies any history of migraines. She will occasionally get a tension headache which she will treat with Ibuprofen. She has never had a headache that has lasted this long. She denies any trauma to her head. She denies any sick contacts. She denies any recent antibiotic use.  She is very concerned about her symptoms.

PMH:
Past Medical History: Hypertension. The patient recently had a colonoscopy screening which was normal. 
Current medications: HCTZ 25mg PO daily
Allergies: None
Vaccinations: Received Childhood vaccinations.
Women's health: The patient cannot remember when her last pap smear was done.
Family History:
Her father has a history of Hypertension and died of a stroke. Her mother has no health problems and is still living.

Social History:
Marital/Family: She is married with one child. She lives in Gaithersburg, Maryland.
Personal habits: The patient denies smoking. She lives an active lifestyle. She drinks alcohol socially. She denies use of illegal drugs or misuse of medications.
Occupation/educational: She works as a public relations specialist. She is a college graduate with a BS in communication.
Recreational: The patient enjoys to bike and hike. She loves the outdoors and spends as much time as she is able.

Review of systems:
General: See HPI
Skin: See HPI
HEENT: Negative
Cardiorespiratory: Negative
Gastrointestinal: See HPI
Genitourinary: Negative
Neuropsychiatric: See HPI

Generalized Weakness.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Generalized weakness
History of present Illness:
A previously healthy 30-year-old female with a PMH of asthma presents to your office complaining of progressive generalized weakness that has been occurring for the past 3 months. Her symptoms worsen at the end of the day, and her weakness improves with rest. She reports occasional blurry vision with improvement after closing and resting her eyes. She also notes difficulty chewing food and swallowing recently. She has also been experiencing dyspnea with strenuous exercise, but it is now refractory to her albuterol inhaler.

Past Medical History: She broke her arm when she was 14 which required an open reduction and pin placement. 
Current medications: Birth control, multi-vitamin
Allergies: Penicillin (rash and shortness of breath)
Vaccinations: Received Childhood vaccinations.
Women's health: She contracted gonorrhea from a sexual partner at age 20 which was successfully treated. She had a LEEP procedure done due to an abnormal pap smear 6 years ago. Her last pap smear was done 3 years ago which was normal. 
Family History: Her father has a history of congestive heart failure and is status post two myocardial infarctions. Her mother has hypothyroidism. Her grandfather died of a stroke. 

Social History:
           Marital/Family: The patient is single. She is currently living and is sexually active with her boyfriend of 2 years. 
           Personal habits: She states that she drinks occasionally. The patient smokes cigars with her boyfriend on occasion when going out.
Occupation/educational: She works as a newspaper editor for a local newspaper. 
Recreational: She enjoys reading and breeds dogs. 

Review of systems:
General: See HPI
Skin: Negative
HEENT: See HPI
Cardiorespiratory: Negative
Gastrointestinal: Negative
Genitourinary:  Negative
Neuropsychiatric: See HPI

Burning During Urination.history:
Initial History

Reason(s) for visit: Burning during urination
History of present Illness:
The patient, a 19 year old college freshman, presents to your office stating that he has a 5/10, sharp pain when he urinates. The pain stops when urination is complete. The pain began 3 days ago and has been progressively getting worse. He also recently developed testicular pain and pain with sex, especially with ejaculation. He is sexually active with his girlfriend of 6 months, and although they typically use condoms, they occasionally have unprotected sex. He also confirms chills, lethargy, a subjective fever, decreased appetite, but denies nausea and vomiting. Tylenol has improved his pain and fever. 

Past Medical History: Fractured ankle.
Current medications: Tylenol 500mg as needed
Allergies: None
Vaccinations: He received his childhood vaccinations.
Family History: The patient's father has hypertension. His mother has hypothyroidism. 

Social History: 
The patient is a college freshman, and had his first sexual encounter at age 15. He states that he never had a sexually transmitted infection before. He is a social drinker and has experimented with marijuana in the past. He denies any other illicit drug use. He enjoys rock climbing.

Review of systems:
General: See HPI
Skin: Negative
HEENT: Negative
Cardiorespiratory: Negative
Gastrointestinal: Negative
Genitourinary:  See HPI
Neuropsychiatric: Negative
